Title: Imbalanced Lignin Biosynthesis Promotes the Sexual Reproduction of Homothallic Oomycete Pathogens

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: S-lignin (syringyl lignin) branch; hydroxycinnamoyl/monolignol methylation branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream branch step in monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Sinapaldehyde-derived syringyl monolignol products; promotes sinapoyl malate accumulation and prevents hydroxyferuloyl malate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-methylates 5-hydroxylated phenylpropanoid intermediates in the syringyl lignin branch, contributing to S-lignin formation and proper hydroxycinnamoyl ester balance
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Controls precursor balance that determines accumulation of hydroxyferuloyl malate, a metabolite affecting oomycete sexual reproduction
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for lignin composition; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Phenylpropanoid; hydroxycinnamoyl malate ester; lignin-related soluble phenolic
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: 2-O-5-hydroxyferuloyl-L-malate weakens oomycete mycelial vigor and promotes sexual reproduction/oospore formation; sinapoyl malate is a major soluble hydroxycinnamate ester in Arabidopsis leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and cotyledons of Arabidopsis seedlings/plantlets
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss or reduction of COMT1 decreases sinapoyl malate, causes accumulation of 2-O-5-hydroxyferuloyl-L-malate, reduces asexual sporulation of Hyaloperonospora arabidopsidis, increases oomycete sexual reproduction, and generally increases susceptibility to several bacterial and fungal pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two Arabidopsis comt1 mutant alleles showed reduced/absent COMT1 transcript and altered lignin-related phenylpropanoid metabolism. 2. comt1 mutants accumulated 2-O-5-hydroxyferuloyl-L-malate, which was undetectable in wild type, while sinapoyl malate levels decreased strongly. 3. H. arabidopsidis produced fewer conidiospores but more oospores on comt1 mutants, without major change in pathogen biomass. 4. Haustorial stability was impaired in mutant tissues. 5. Complementation with poplar PtOMT1 partially restored COMT activity/S-lignin and largely rescued pathogen phenotypes. 6. Synthetic 2-O-5-hydroxyferuloyl-L-malate promoted sexual reproduction of a homothallic oomycete in vitro, supporting causality between COMT1-dependent metabolic imbalance and the pathogen phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout and knockdown mutants (comt1a, comt1b); genetic complementation with PtOMT1; RT-PCR/qRT-PCR; promoter-trap GUS expression; HPLC metabolite profiling with synthetic standards; pathogen infection phenotyping; microscopy of haustoria/oospores; in vitro bioassay with synthetic hydroxycinnamoyl malates
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: COMT1 native promoter monitored by promoter-trap GUS; constitutive promoter used for PtOMT1 complementation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in lignifying tissues including vascular tissues, veins, root central cylinder, leaf primordia, cotyledon distal focus, and root cap columella cells; induced locally in parenchyma cells near invading hyphae and in nematode-induced gall/giant cells upon pathogen infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Restricted under normal conditions to lignifying tissues; pathogen-inducible in mesophyll, cotyledons, hypocotyl, and root gall tissues close to infection sites
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pathogen infection by Hyaloperonospora arabidopsidis, Meloidogyne incognita, Botrytis cinerea, Alternaria brassicicola, Blumeria graminis, Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato, and Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: COMT1 downregulation may increase susceptibility to several fungal and bacterial pathogens and may stimulate oomycete sexual reproduction, potentially increasing long-term disease persistence and evolution of new infection traits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study’s metabolism-relevant causal signal is not lignin amount per se but the metabolic disequilibrium caused by COMT1 loss, especially the altered sinapoyl malate/hydroxyferuloyl malate ratio. COMT1 was not required for resistance to avirulent H. arabidopsidis isolate Noco2 and did not alter SA/JA marker induction in the tested interaction.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream branch step in monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Methylated syringyl-branch intermediates supporting S-lignin and sinapoyl malate restoration
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Heterologous O-methyltransferase used to complement Arabidopsis COMT1 deficiency and restore proper phenylpropanoid flux
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Restores metabolic balance and rescues altered hydroxycinnamoyl malate profile affecting oomycete reproduction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Restoration of normal metabolite balance correlates with reduced oomycete sexual reproduction and restored asexual sporulation tendency
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive expression of PtOMT1 in comt1a partially/completely complements COMT deficiency, restoring near-normal COMT activity, S-lignin, sinapoyl malate predominance, and near-wild-type H. arabidopsidis phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtOMT1 was expressed in Arabidopsis comt1a as a complementation transgene to avoid silencing seen with Arabidopsis COMT1 overexpression. 2. The CpOMT14 line recovered almost normal COMT activity and S-lignin. 3. CpOMT14 restored sinapoyl malate as the major hydroxycinnamoyl malate and reduced hydroxyferuloyl malate accumulation. 4. Complementation largely rescued reduced conidiospore production and elevated oospore formation seen in comt1 mutants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic complementation; RT-PCR; HPLC metabolite profiling; pathogen sporulation/oospore assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenic constitutive expression in Arabidopsis comt1a complementation line CpOMT14
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a functional ortholog for complementation rather than as an endogenous focal gene regulating nutrient metabolism.
